description: Male Rufous-sided Towhee with white "check box" at base of folded primaries and white extending along lower edge of folded primaries. Also, three tertials had white tips. No white on back/scaps nor on wing coverts as in Spotted Towhee. Tail spots large, nearly reaching tip of undertail coverts. Upper parts and hood black. Sides orange-rufous. Vent and undertail coverts pale yellow buff. Head rounded and bill smaller (particurly smaller based) than Spotted Towhee. Call zhe-wee, second syllable louder, typical of this species.
